message:
fix(websocket): add timeout to close handshake read loop

Both WebSocket client and server close handshake loops called
read_more().await without a timeout, which could block indefinitely if
the peer stopped sending frames during the close sequence. This left
connections hanging until the OS-level TCP timeout.

Fix applied identically to both client.rs and server.rs:
- Check if deadline has already passed before attempting each read
- Wrap read_more() in crate::time::timeout() with remaining duration
  calculated from the close deadline
- Obtain current time from the Cx timer driver when available,
  falling back to wall_now() when no driver is present
- On timeout elapsed: force_close with going_away reason and break
- On read returning 0 bytes: force_close as before
- On read error: propagate immediately

This ensures the close handshake respects its deadline even when the
peer is unresponsive, preventing resource leaks from zombie connections.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
